attributes.description: Alerts when average database connections exceed a threshold. Uses the Average statistic per AWS recommended alarms. CloudWatch does not publish max_connections — set the threshold against your engine limit and normal baseline.
attributes.name: [AWS RDS OTel] Database connections high

investigation_guide.blob:
## Database Connections High

### Triage
1. Compare average connections to the engine `max_connections` setting (not in CloudWatch).
2. Check for connection leaks — idle sessions accumulating over time.
3. Review application deploys or traffic spikes that increased connection demand.
4. Inspect `FreeableMemory` — each connection consumes memory.

### Mitigation
- Enable connection pooling (PgBouncer, RDS Proxy, application pool).
- Fix connection leaks in application code.
- Increase `max_connections` only if memory allows; prefer pooling first.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.rds.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/RDS"
    AND MetricName == "DatabaseConnections"
    AND stat == "Average"
| STATS avg_connections = AVG(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DatabaseConnections`)
    BY DBInstanceIdentifier, cloud.region
// Average connection count — set relative to max_connections and baseline (default: 100)
| WHERE avg_connections > 100
| SORT avg_connections DESC
